methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Karman Industries is a Seed-stage climate and energy company founded by David Tearse and Chiranjeev Kalra. The target operates within a sector comprising one public comparable (Lenza, ticker LNZA) and eight private peers including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen. However, the private peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage datapoints to support a robust peer-median valuation method; only one narrow-filter comparable was identified, rendering peer triangulation inapplicable.

The analysis therefore relies on stage-progression methodology. Using total capital raised of $11.5M as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression, the method implies a valuation of $29M. This single-method approach establishes a central estimate with modest variance: a low bound of $24M (incorporating downside sensitivity) and a high bound of $33M (upside case).

The $24M–$33M range, centered at $29M, reflects the valuation supported by stage-progression mechanics. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 2.5x step-up multiplier for this sector, the absence of direct peer-valuation anchors, and the company's progress metrics relative to typical Seed-stage climate tech milestones. Additional comparable transactions or updated revenue/traction data would improve conviction.
